question_text: What happens when you rub two insulating materials together?

options.label: A
options.text: Electrons move from one material to the other, creating a charge imbalance

options.label: C
options.text: Heat energy is converted into electrical current

options.label: D
options.text: The materials lose all their electrons

correct_answer: Electrons move from one material to the other, creating a charge imbalance
explanation: When two insulating materials are rubbed together, electrons transfer from one material to the other. The material that loses electrons becomes positively charged (electron deficient), while the material that gains electrons becomes negatively charged (electron excess). This creates a charge imbalance and is the basis of static electricity.
